items.headline: Tech Sell-Off Deepens Amid Geopolitical Tensions; Major Indices Close Lower
items.summary: U.S. equities are facing significant headwinds today, with a deepening global sell-off in technology and semiconductor stocks. Investor sentiment is further weighed down by escalating tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which has driven oil prices higher. All major indices registered declines, with the Nasdaq Composite leading the losses. Energy and Real Estate sectors showed resilience, while Communication Services and Technology lagged. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Netflix Plunges on Weak Guidance
items.summary: Shares of streaming giant Netflix ($NFLX) tumbled over 7% after the company issued disappointing third-quarter revenue guidance, signaling slowing growth.

items.headline: Intuitive Surgical Falls on Outlook Concerns
items.summary: Intuitive Surgical ($ISRG) tumbled 11.4% in early trading following a weaker-than-expected outlook and recall concerns.

items.headline: Nvidia Down Amid Broader Chip Sell-Off
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) shares declined approximately 3.4% as the global sell-off in semiconductor and AI-related stocks intensified, driven by valuation concerns.

items.headline: Travelers Companies Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: The Travelers Companies ($TRV) surged 7.4% in early trading, providing a notable upside move amidst a down market.

items.headline: Abbott Laboratories Beats Earnings Estimates
items.summary: Abbott Laboratories ($ABT) saw its stock rise 10.7% after reporting second-quarter 2026 earnings and revenues that surpassed Zacks Consensus Estimates.

items.headline: SK Hynix Recovers After Initial Plunge
items.summary: U.S.-listed shares of SK Hynix ($SKHY) demonstrated significant volatility, initially plunging before reversing losses to trade up 8.16% by midday, amidst a turbulent week for chip stocks.

items.headline: Energy and Utilities Lead While Tech and Communications Lag
items.summary: Today's market saw Energy and Utilities sectors leading the gains, alongside Real Estate and Consumer Staples. In contrast, the Technology, Semiconductor, Communication Services, and Consumer Discretionary sectors experienced significant declines as investors rotated out of high-growth AI-related stocks.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Escalating geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, continue to weigh on global markets and have pushed crude oil prices significantly higher. WTI crude gained 3.7% to $81.88 a barrel.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Fall, Philly Fed Index Rises
items.summary: New economic data showed initial jobless claims decreased to 208,000 for the week ended July 11, falling below estimates. The Philly Fed Index for July also rose significantly to 41.1, indicating stronger manufacturing activity.

items.headline: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple ($AAPL) has reclaimed its position as the world's most valuable company, surpassing Nvidia ($NVDA) in intraday trading, with Apple's market capitalization reaching approximately $4.9 trillion.

items.headline: Meta Reportedly in Talks for $10 Billion Anthropic AI Deal
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) is reportedly in discussions to lease computing power to AI firm Anthropic in a potential $10 billion deal, a move that could bolster Meta's AI strategy.
